# Delete the deploy-target redeploy operator-page ã record (no human to page in cloud)

## Context

The remaining human-page path in the recovery actuator. `RecoveryActuatorService` still dispatches an **operator page** for a deploy-target that needs redeploy (`RecoveryActuatorService.mjs` ~L597ã616: builds `{operatorPageTarget}` + calls `pageDispatcher`), and `'page'` is still in `DEFAULT_ACTIONS` (L17). In an operatorless cloud deployment there is no human to page ã this is the last surviving smoke-detector terminal in the lifecycle actuator.

#14201/PR #14229 delivered the lifecycle core (config-drift ã `warm-provider` reconfigure heal; `escalateDiagnosis` ã `recordDiagnosis`; producers ã `record`/act). This is the distinct **deploy-target / B1** page path it deliberately left, because it's woven through the deploy-target status mapping (`'escalated'`) + the attempt-cap alarm-only path.

## The Fix

- Route the deploy-target redeploy page ã `appendHealEvent` (record-with-diagnosis, the un-resolvable terminal ã the actuator cannot exec arbitrary deployment code, so an un-auto-redeployable target records, never pages).
- Remove `'page'` from `DEFAULT_ACTIONS` + the `pageDispatcher_` config + the `page` field in the action outcome.
- The deploy-target / attempt-cap `status: 'escalated'` ã `'recorded'` (+ `getLedgerStatus`); update the "deploy-target redeploy pages" + "attempt cap" specs to assert a ledger record, not a page.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No `pageDispatcher` call / no `operatorPageTarget` / no `'page'` action remains in `RecoveryActuatorService`.
- [ ] Deploy-target un-auto-redeployable ã records to `healEventLedgerStore`.
- [ ] Specs assert the record terminal; orchestrator service specs green.

## Related
Completes #14201 AC #3 (no human-page path remains). Depends-on the #14229 merge.

Authored by Grace (Claude Opus 4.8). Origin session 090a68e6-1a28-4b20-a5fd-842ebac3e729.

**Routing:** I added `not-code-ready` + `deferred-by-design` so this does not appear as a claimable implementation lane while its prerequisite PR is still failing. Once #14229 is green/merged, remove those labels and this can proceed as the deploy-target page-path cleanup slice.
